questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The number of accidents have increased.
options: A number of accidents has increased.
The number of accidents has increased.
The number of accidents is increasing.
The number of accidents have increased.
questions.solution: The number of accidents has increased.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The dissolved oxygen content in clean river water is approximately:
questions.solution: Clean river water DO = 6-8 mg/L (saturation ~9 mg/L at 20°C).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Admixtures that accelerate the setting time of concrete are called:
questions.solution: Accelerators speed up hydration and early strength gain.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has G = 2.7, e = 0.8, S = 0.5. The water content is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lateral ties in an RCC column primarily prevent:
questions.solution: Ties hold the main bars in position and prevent their lateral buckling.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The COD test measures:
options: Oxygen equivalent of organic matter oxidizable by K₂Cr₂O₇
questions.solution: COD = chemical oxidation with K₂Cr₂O₇/H₂SO₄ (includes both biodegradable and non-biodegradable).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A concrete with 28-day strength of 40 MPa is classified as:
questions.solution: M40 = characteristic compressive strength of 40 MPa at 28 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seepage force per unit volume in a soil mass is:
questions.solution: Seepage force/vol = iγ_w where i = hydraulic gradient.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IS 456 limit-state design, the maximum compressive strain in concrete at the extreme fibre at the ultimate limit state is:
questions.solution: The limiting concrete strain in flexure is 0.0035; 0.002 is the strain at the onset of the constant-stress (rectangular) portion of the stress block.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The detention time of a tank of volume 200 m³ treating a flow of 100 m³/h is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The split tensile strength of concrete is approximately:
questions.solution: Split tensile f_ct ~ 0.7√f_ck (IS 456). Flexural tensile f_cr = 0.7√f_ck.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Rankine active earth pressure coefficient Ka for a soil with φ = 30° is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The limiting moment of resistance of a singly reinforced beam with Fe415 steel is:
questions.solution: For Fe415, Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d².

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The treatment process for removing hardness from water is:
questions.solution: Lime-soda softens water by precipitating Ca and Mg as carbonates/hydroxides.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The fineness modulus of sand for concrete should be between:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The phreatic line in an earth dam is also known as:
questions.solution: Phreatic line = uppermost flow line in an earth dam seepage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a singly reinforced section (Fe415, M30 concrete, width 300 mm, effective depth 500 mm), the limiting moment of resistance (Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d²) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a balanced (limiting) Fe415 section the moment of resistance is fixed by the coefficient 0.138 in $f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$.
**Formula.** $M_{u,lim}=0.138\,f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$
**Given.** f_ck = 30 MPa, b = 300 mm, d = 500 mm.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.138\times30\times300\times500^2}{10^6}$ kNm.
**Answer.** $M_{u,lim}\approx 310.5$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The allowable limit of nitrate in drinking water as per IS 10500 is:
questions.solution: NO3 limit: 45 mg/L (max 100 mg/L where no alternative).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The bulking of sand is caused by:
options: Surface tension of water films separating sand particles
questions.solution: Bulking = increase in volume due to moisture film on sand particles.

questions.stem: The critical hydraulic gradient for quick sand condition is approximately:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The activated sludge process is used for the treatment of:
questions.solution: The activated sludge process is an aerobic biological treatment for sewage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A concrete is said to be 'self-compacting' if the slump flow is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 0 kPa and friction angle φ = 30°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 80 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 0 kPa, φ = 30°, σ = 80 kPa.
**Working.** $=0+80\times\tan 30^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=46.19$ kPa.

questions.stem: A beam has overall depth 600 mm, clear cover 25 mm and 16 mm main bars. The effective depth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The filtration rate in a rapid sand filter is approximately:
questions.solution: RSF filtration rate = 4.5-6 m/h = 4500-6000 L/m²/h. Slow sand = 100-200 L/m²/h.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Segregation in concrete is:
options: Separation of coarse aggregate from the mortar
questions.solution: Segregation = coarse aggregate settling or separating.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Standard Penetration Test (SPT) gives the soil's:
questions.solution: The SPT reports the blow count N used to estimate relative density and strength.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In limit state design of a column, the assumed stress-strain curve for concrete is:
questions.solution: Concrete stress block: parabolic up to 0.002, constant 0.447 f_ck up to 0.0035.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The WHO guideline for fluoride in drinking water is:

questions.stem: The grade of cement 53 indicates:
options: Compressive strength of 53 MPa at 28 days

questions.stem: A beam has overall depth 450 mm, clear cover 30 mm and 16 mm main bars. The effective depth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Loss due to relaxation of steel is higher for:
questions.solution: Relaxation increases with initial stress/UTS ratio (non-linear).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The maximum uni-axial eccentricity ratio (e/D) for a column under axial load with bending is:
options: 0.2 (for limit state design of short columns under axial load only)
questions.solution: e/D ≤ 0.2 for interaction diagram regime shift.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A riveted joint in which the rivets are arranged in more than one row is called:
questions.solution: Multiple-row = zigzag or chain riveting for greater strength.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The superelevation for a design speed of 50 km/h and radius 150 m (e = V²/127R) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The modern equivalent of the plumb bob in a total station is:
questions.solution: Laser plummet replaces plumb bob for centering over a station.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The design shear strength of concrete τ_c depends on:
options: Percentage of tensile reinforcement and grade of concrete
questions.solution: τ_c depends on pt = 100Ast/bd and concrete grade M (from Table 19 IS 456).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 4 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 10 kN/m over its entire length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a cantilever produces its maximum moment at the fixed support.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{2}$
**Given.** w = 10 kN/m, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{10\times4^2}{2}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=80$ kNm at the fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge over a rectangular weir (Cd = 0.62) of length 4 m under a head of 0.3 m is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Discharge over a sharp-crested rectangular weir varies with the 3/2 power of the head.
**Formula.** $Q=\tfrac23 C_d\sqrt{2g}\,L\,H^{3/2}$
**Given.** C_d = 0.62, L = 4 m, H = 0.3 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac23\times0.62\times\sqrt{2\times9.81}\times4\times0.3^{1.5}$.
**Answer.** $Q\approx 1.2$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a sieve analysis, the cumulative percentage passing through a 4.75 mm sieve is 60%. The percentage of gravel in the soil is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever 3 m long carries a 4 kN load at its free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: Max BM at fixed end = W × L = 4 × 3 = 12 kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a sewerage system, the minimum velocity to prevent silting is:
questions.solution: Self-cleansing velocity = 0.6 m/s for sewage, 0.45 m/s for storm water (min).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The length of transition curve is governed by:
options: Rate of change of centrifugal acceleration, rate of superelevation, and IRC empirical formula
questions.solution: L = max of: v³/(CR), e×B, and IRC empirical (2.7v²/R).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bearing measured from magnetic north is called:
questions.solution: MB = measured from magnetic north with compass.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a circular column with helical reinforcement, the strength is:
options: 1.05 times the strength of tied column (due to confinement)
questions.solution: Helical reinforcement provides confinement → 1.05 × strength of tied column.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 8 m carries a central point load of 10 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 10 kN, L = 8 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{10\times8}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=20$ kNm at mid-span.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cavitation occurs when the pressure in a liquid:
questions.solution: Cavitation = vapour bubble formation when local pressure < vapour pressure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 20 kPa and friction angle φ = 35°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 100 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 20 kPa, φ = 35°, σ = 100 kPa.
**Working.** $=20+100\times\tan 35^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=90.02$ kPa.

questions.stem: The maximum deflection of a simply supported beam with UDL w over span L is:

questions.stem: If BOD₅ at 20°C is 200 mg/L and the reaction rate constant k = 0.1/day, the ultimate BOD is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In flexible pavements, the stresses are:
options: Highest at the surface and decrease with depth
questions.solution: Load spreads with depth; maximum stress at surface contact.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The optimum binder content in bituminous concrete is determined at:
questions.solution: OBC at 4% air voids for dense bituminous macadam (as per MORTH).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of length 2 m carries a 8 kN load at its free end (E = 200 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴). The tip deflection (WL³/3EI) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An end-loaded cantilever has tip deflection $W L^3/3EI$.
**Formula.** $\delta=\tfrac{W L^3}{3 E I}$
**Given.** W = 8 kN, L = 2 m, E = 200 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{8\times10^3\times2^3}{3\times200\times10^9\times16\times10^{-6}}$ m.
**Answer.** $\delta\approx 6.7$ mm.

questions.stem: The section modulus of a rectangular section 100 mm wide and 200 mm deep is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Comparing a three-hinged and a two-hinged arch, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: A three-hinged arch is statically indeterminate to the first degree.
Support settlement induces no stress in a three-hinged arch.
A three-hinged arch is free of temperature stresses.
A two-hinged arch is indeterminate to the first degree.
questions.solution: A three-hinged arch is statically determinate (the third hinge supplies the extra equation), so calling it indeterminate is false.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In plastic design, the collapse mechanism is formed when the number of plastic hinges equals:
questions.solution: r + 1 plastic hinges needed for collapse (mechanism method).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the slope-deflection method, the primary unknowns are the:
questions.solution: Slope-deflection treats the unknown joint displacements (rotations/translations) as primary unknowns.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 8 m carries a point load of 15 kN at the free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: BM_max = PL = 15×8 = 120 kNm at fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 8 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 15 kN/m over its full span. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a simply supported beam peaks at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{8}$
**Given.** w = 15 kN/m, L = 8 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{15\times8^2}{8}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=120$ kNm.

questions.solution: Shape factor = plastic modulus / elastic section modulus = 1.5 for a rectangle.

questions.stem: A cantilever of span 5 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 10 kN/m over its entire length. The maximum bending moment is:

**Answer.** $M_{max}=125$ kNm at the fixed end.

questions.solution: Distribution factor = K/ΣK for each member meeting at the joint.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 15 kPa and friction angle φ = 25°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 60 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 15 kPa, φ = 25°, σ = 60 kPa.
**Working.** $=15+60\times\tan 25^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=42.98$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the same wall, the passive thrust if the fill height is 4 m is:
questions.solution: Kp = 1/Ka = 3. Pp = 0.5γH²Kp = 0.5×17×16×3 = 408 kN/m. Hmm that doesn't match. Let me recalc: 0.5×17×16×3 = 408.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3 m clay layer (Cc = 0.4, e₀ = 1) under an initial effective stress of 100 kPa receives a 60 kPa increment. The primary consolidation settlement is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Primary consolidation of a normally consolidated clay follows the logarithmic compression law.
**Formula.** $S_c=\tfrac{C_c H}{1+e_0}\log_{10}\tfrac{\sigma_0+\Delta\sigma}{\sigma_0}$
**Given.** C_c = 0.4, H = 3 m, e₀ = 1, σ₀ = 100 kPa, Δσ = 60 kPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.4\times3000}{1+1}\log_{10}\tfrac{100+60}{100}$ mm.
**Answer.** $S_c\approx 122.5$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principal stresses at a point are 50 MPa and 10 MPa. The normal stress on the plane carrying the maximum shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** On the plane of maximum shear the normal stress equals the centre of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\sigma_n=\tfrac{\sigma_1+\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 50 MPa, σ₃ = 10 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{50+10}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_n=30$ MPa.

figure.caption: Centre of Mohr's circle = mean normal stress

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 15 kPa and friction angle φ = 30°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 120 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 15 kPa, φ = 30°, σ = 120 kPa.
**Working.** $=15+120\times\tan 30^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=84.28$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In triangulation, the strength of a figure is measured by:
options: The R value (accuracy of side computation)
questions.solution: Strength of figure = R = (D² - d²)/(D²) where D = number of directions, d = number of unknowns.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The preconsolidation pressure of a soil can be determined by:
questions.solution: Casagrande's graphical method on e-logσ' curve gives preconsolidation pressure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The degree of static indeterminacy of a single-bay, single-storey portal frame with both bases fixed is:
questions.solution: A fixed-base single-bay portal frame is indeterminate to the third degree.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The second theorem of Castigliano is:
questions.solution: Castigliano P1: ∂U/∂P_i = δ_i. Also ∂U/∂M = θ at point of M application.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a PSC slab, using draped tendons provides:
options: Varying eccentricity to match the bending moment diagram
questions.solution: Draped = parabolic profile with max eccentricity where BM is max.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Water flows at 1 m/s through a pipe of diameter 0.3 m. The discharge is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A strip footing of width 2 m is founded at depth 2 m. Soil: c=20 kPa, γ=18 kN/m³, Nc=30.1, Nq=17.7, Nγ=15.7. The ultimate bearing capacity (Terzaghi) is:
questions.solution: $q_u = cN_c + \gamma D_f N_q + 0.5\gamma B N_\gamma = 1521.8$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a singly reinforced section (Fe415, M25 concrete, width 230 mm, effective depth 550 mm), the limiting moment of resistance (Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d²) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a balanced (limiting) Fe415 section the moment of resistance is fixed by the coefficient 0.138 in $f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$.
**Formula.** $M_{u,lim}=0.138\,f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$
**Given.** f_ck = 25 MPa, b = 230 mm, d = 550 mm.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.138\times25\times230\times550^2}{10^6}$ kNm.
**Answer.** $M_{u,lim}\approx 240$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries two equal 10 kN point loads placed 1 m from each support. The maximum bending moment (constant between the loads) is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two equal symmetric loads give equal reactions; between the loads the bending moment is constant at $R\cdot a$.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=P\,a$
**Given.** P = 10 kN, a = 1 m, span = 6 m.
**Working.** Each reaction $=10$ kN; $M_{max}=10\times1$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=10$ kNm.
